publishedAbstract: Ceph Cluster in "`HEALTH_WARN`" with "`1 clients failing to respond to capability release`". The issue where a CephFS Kernel Client is failing to respond to capability release is a symptom with many use cases. This Playbook KCS Solution aggregates all known critical issues into one place so Red Hat Support can highlight clearly the minimum versions for OCP, CoreOS, and Ceph. **Critical KCS Solutions which have been remediated**: KCS Solution Link | Title | Fix in Ceph or Kernel |------------------|----------------------|------------------------| KCS Solution [7048761](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/7048761) | MDS has the cluster in a "HEALTH_WARN" with "1 clients failing to respond to capability release". | Both | KCS Solution [7067294](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/7067294) | RHEL/RHOCP: RIP:__list_del_entry_valid.cold- "ceph_drop_caps_for_unlink". | Kernel | KCS Solution [7083772](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/7083772) | MDS allocates memory until OOM or memory exhaustion or "1 clients failing to respond to capability release". | Both | KCS Solution [7092285](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/7092285) | Kernel Panic: "kernel BUG at fs/ceph/addr.c:97!"- "RIP: 0010:ceph_dirty_folio+0x183/0x190 [ceph]". | Kernel | KCS Solution [7121871](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/7121871) | Kernel panic with list corruption detected during large folio migration. | Kernel | KCS Solution [7128341](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/7128341) | CephFS: System Deadlock during File Move Operations on Shared Volumes. | Kernel | KCS Solution [7142975](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/7142975) | CephFS Shallow Clone does not work as expected when an RWX clone is in progress. | Ceph MDS | KCS Solution [7142990](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/7142990) | CephFS clone operation (in progress) is looping between start and error states. | Ceph MDS |   Example: ~~~-bash 5.1 $ ceph-s cluster: id: 7153xxxx-Redacted-Cluster-ID-yyyy49cf8043 health: HEALTH_WARN 1 clients failing to respond to capability release services: mon: 5 daemons, quorum edon-1,edon-2,edon-3,edon-4,edon-5 (age 23h) mgr: edon-5(active, since 11d), standbys: edon-1, edon-4, edon-2, edon-3 mds: 1/1 daemons up, 1 standby, 1 hot standby osd: 768 osds: 768 up (since 2d), 768 in (since 2d); 268 remapped pgs rgw: 6 daemons active (6 hosts, 2 zones) data: volumes: 1/1 healthy pools: 45 pools, 25841 pgs objects: 4.59G objects, 507 TiB usage: 1.8 PiB used, 2.0 PiB / 3.8 PiB avail pgs: 25836 active+clean 4 active+clean+scrubbing+deep 1 active+clean+scrubbing io: client: 221 MiB/s rd, 88 MiB/s wr, 7.41k op/s rd, 5.36k op/s wr-bash 5.1 $ ceph health detail HEALTH_WARN 1 clients failing to respond to capability release [WRN] MDS_CLIENT_LATE_RELEASE: 1 clients failing to respond to capability release mds.edon-4(mds.0): Client client-46.MyBigCo.org failing to respond to capability release client_id: 146372720 ~~~
publishedTitle: Ceph/ODF:  [Playbook]  Ceph Cluster in `HEALTH_WARN` with "1 clients failing to respond to capability release".
